explanation: The requirement specifies that the controller must implement six modes: Start-Up Mode, Self-Test Mode, VentilationOff (also known as StandbyMode), Pressure Controlled Ventilation Mode (PCVMode), Pressure Support Ventilation Mode (PSVMode), and fail-safe (failSafeMode). The only relevant way to represent this with the provided atomic propositions is to assert that the system must always allow these six modes to be indicated (i.e., possible to be in any one of them). Since no temporal relationship or order between these modes is specified in the requirement, the best structured natural language translation with the available templates is: 'always satisfy (StartUpMode | SelfTestMode | StandbyMode | PCVMode | PSVMode | failSafeMode)'. This captures the intent that the system must at every time be in one of the allowed modes, which is a common way of expressing mode completeness in requirements.
